title: Reasons to Stay Alive
author: Matt Haig
subline: What depression is, and what it isn't.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A simple list: 12 numbered lines stacked in the top 2/3. Most are 'reasons' (graphite). ONE is RED and italic — the most important reason. Title sits below. -->
  <g transform="translate(126 380)" fill="none" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif">
    <!-- Numbered list items -->
    <g>
      <g transform="translate(0 0)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">01</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">The first cup of coffee in the morning.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 50)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">02</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">The sound of rain on a window at night.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 100)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">03</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">A dog that wags its tail when you come home.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 150)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">04</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">Old books, yellowed and foxed.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 200)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">05</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">The smell of cut grass.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 250)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">06</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">A long phone call with an old friend.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 300)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">07</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">Waking up and realizing you're okay.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 350)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">08</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">The first warm day of spring.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 400)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">09</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">A song you forgot you loved.</text>
      </g>
      <!-- Item 10 — RED, italic, slightly larger (the most important reason) -->
      <g transform="translate(0 460)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.2em">10</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="22" font-weight="300" font-style="italic" fill="#fc1c46">You are loved, and you don't know it yet.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 510)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">11</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">The next chapter of any book you're reading.</text>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(0 560)">
        <text x="0" y="0" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">12</text>
        <text x="80" y="0" font-size="18" font-weight="400" fill="#cccccc">Tomorrow.</text>
      </g>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: The book IS a list. A literal 12-item numbered list fills the top 2/3 — small ordinary things, the 'reasons' Haig found to stay alive. Item 10 is the only one in RED, italic, slightly larger: 'You are loved, and you don't know it yet.' The cover is the book's argument, made into a single list. Title sits in the bottom 1/3, weight 300 whisper, mixed-case. Brand '60M' in the top-left (the 60M+ people with depression globally); pill 'DAY 1' at the top — the start of recovery.
